Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Okay the user wants me to outline the thought process behind generating the questions for the property onboarding process Let me start by breaking down the original query The user provided a detailed process description and asked for targeted questions to clarify and improve understanding

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to analyze the given process to identify areas where details might be missing The process has several stages initial contact documentation review property manager assignment inspections marketing tenant screening and final audits Each stage likely has decision points or exceptions that arent fully explained

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I should consider the key elements the user mentioned missing details decision criteria roles timing and exception handling For each part of the process I can brainstorm questions that probe deeper For example in the documentation phase what happens if documents are incomplete How are updates tracked

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Next think about the stakeholders involved landlords property coordinators managers inspectors etc Questions about communication and responsibilities would help clarify roles Also timing between steps isnt specified so asking about duration and deadlines makes sense

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Exceptions are crucial The user mentioned special cases like custom leases or unique properties I need to include questions about how deviations are managed and who approves them

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should avoid technical or SQLrelated questions focusing instead on conceptual clarity Openended questions that encourage detailed responses are better than yesno ones

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Finally structure the questions to follow the process flow ensuring each major stage is covered Group them into themes like documentation assignments inspections marketing tenant selection and audits to keep the inquiry organized Make sure the questions are clear and directly address the users goals of uncovering gaps and understanding decision making
